name: Cream (food medium)
basis: per 100 g fresh weight; representative of fluid cream at ~36% fat (heavy whipping cream, USDA FDC 170857). FooDB 'Cream' is a generic, unquantified record; heavy cream is used as the anchor and the growth-relevant fraction (lactose, organic acids, amino acids, minerals, vitamins) is the focus.

components.name: water
components.evidence: aqueous serum phase carrying lactose, citrate, minerals and free amino acids

components.name: lactose
components.source: USDA FDC 170857; Arla Lactofree (cream lactose ~3-4 g/100g decreasing with fat)
components.evidence: the essentially sole fermentable sugar of cream and the primary carbohydrate carbon/energy source; MW 342.3, density ~1.0 g/mL used for mM

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: milk-composition literature (free glucose in bovine milk ~0.03-0.07 g/L)
components.evidence: only trace free glucose exists in the serum; not a quantitatively meaningful sugar in unsweetened cream

components.name: citric acid
components.source: derived from bovine-milk citrate ~0.16-0.18 g/100g (Supplee & Bellis, J Biol Chem 1921, S0021925818860252; White & Davies J Dairy Res) scaled by cream aqueous/serum fraction
components.evidence: citrate resides in the milk serum; it is the dominant native organic acid of sweet cream and the main FooDB omission. Value = milk serum citrate x cream aqueous fraction (~0.72). MW 192.1

components.name: lactic acid
components.source: dairy-chemistry literature (fresh sweet cream ~0.01-0.02 g/100g; rises to >0.5 g/100g when soured/cultured)
components.evidence: near-absent in fresh/sweet cream, but a defining carbon source once the product is fermented (sour cream); include with note

components.name: orotic acid
components.source: bovine-milk orotic acid ~0.05-0.09 g/L (dairy composition literature)
components.evidence: minor pyrimidine-precursor organic acid characteristic of ruminant milk serum

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: casein + whey; source of the amino-acid profile below

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: protein-derived L-serine (replaces the implausible 'D-Serine' entry in the current record)

components.name: total fat
components.evidence: milkfat triacylglycerols; the dominant mass and energy fraction of cream

components.name: cholesterol
components.evidence: genuine animal sterol of dairy fat - KEPT (removal guidance applies only to plant foods)

components.name: palmitic acid (C16:0)
components.evidence: most abundant saturated FA of milkfat

components.name: myristoleic acid (C14:1)
components.evidence: minor monounsaturated milkfat FA (was in uncovered; genuine, retained)

components.name: gamma-linolenic acid (C18:3 n-6)
components.evidence: trace milkfat PUFA (current 'Gamolenic acid'); present but not quantified

components.name: iodine
components.source: dairy iodine literature (highly variable, ~10-30 ug/100g in milk)
components.evidence: genuine dairy micromineral but not quantified in FDC 170857; present, amount variable

components.name: chloride
components.evidence: serum electrolyte, present but not in FDC panel

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.evidence: present in milk/cream (~1-3 ug/100g) but not in FDC panel; unquantified

components.name: beta-carotene
components.evidence: genuine milkfat carotenoid (provitamin A), gives cream its colour; present, small

remove.name: Sucrose C12H22O11
remove.reason: not native to cream; sucrose occurs only in sweetened cream products. FooDB/generic artifact

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: a plant (Apiaceae) furanocoumarin; biologically implausible in dairy - FooDB predicted, not measured

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: an antimicrobial food additive, not a native nutrient of cream

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: stereochemical artifact / duplicate of L-isoleucine already quantified

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: non-proteinogenic D-enantiomer; milk protein contributes L-serine (mis-entry, replaced by L-serine)

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: trace flavour aldehyde (volatile), not a quantitatively meaningful growth nutrient

remove.name: Nickel
remove.reason: non-nutritive ultratrace contaminant element, not a defining cream nutrient

remove.name: Chromium
remove.reason: ultratrace element, not a quantified/defining cream nutrient - FooDB noise

remove.name: Molybdate
remove.reason: ultratrace element in cream serum; FooDB dump, not a growth-relevant nutrient at cream levels

remove.name: Co2+
remove.reason: free cobalt ion is an ultratrace contaminant; biologically relevant cobalt is bound in B12, already listed

missing_added: citric acid (dominant native organic acid of cream serum - key FooDB omission)
quantified lactose amount (the sole significant sugar)
quantitative amino-acid amounts for the full milk-protein profile
quantitative amounts for all fatty acids, minerals and vitamins
keep_uncovered: beta-Lactose (alpha/beta anomer of lactose; real, folded into 'lactose')
24,25-dihydroxyvitamin D3 (trace vitamin D metabolite; genuine, no BiGG met)
beta-carotene / glycine betaine / biotin (genuine but partly unquantified dairy micronutrients)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170857 (Cream, fluid, heavy whipping) - full macro/amino-acid/fatty-acid/mineral/vitamin panel
FooDB FOOD00650 (Cream) - generic record, tables unpopulated
Supplee & Bellis, Citric acid content of milk and milk products, J Biol Chem 1921 (S0021925818860252)
Arla Lactofree - lactose content of cream by fat level
Dairy-chemistry literature for milk-serum citrate/lactate/orotate partitioning and trace micronutrients
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for microbial growth on cream are lactose (~2.9 g/100g, the only significant sugar) and milk-serum citric acid (~0.13 g/100g, the main FooDB omission); lactic acid is negligible in fresh sweet cream but becomes dominant once the product is cultured/soured. Basis anchored to heavy whipping cream (~36% fat); lower-fat creams have proportionally more serum-phase lactose, citrate, protein and minerals and less fat. Cholesterol is retained as a genuine dairy sterol (plant-food removal rule does not apply).
